subject: Mathematics
title: Fibonacci and the Rabbit Problem
description: A quick, practical guide to Fibonacci and the Rabbit Problem.

hook.text: A 13th-century Italian posed a simple puzzle about rabbits that produced the most famous sequence in math.

buildup.text: Fibonacci's real mission was introducing Hindu-Arabic numerals to Europe in his book Liber Abaci.

discovery.text: The rabbit problem produced 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13—each number the sum of the two before.

twist.text: Fibonacci didn't invent the sequence. Indian mathematicians described it centuries earlier.

climax.text: His book convinced European merchants to abandon Roman numerals. Commerce was never the same.

punchline.text: A rabbit riddle smuggled modern numbers into Europe.

quiz.question: What was Fibonacci's main contribution to European mathematics?
